Based on the initial belief that only the superior rectal artery was important, I've come to understand that all three arteries — superior, middle, and inferior — contribute to the vascular supply of the anal canal. Although the superior rectal artery provides the majority of the blood flow, the middle and inferior rectal arteries also play a role, particularly in certain parts of the anal canal. This realization changes my previous conclusion, as it shows that while the superior artery is predominant, the others cannot be entirely disregarded.
